items.title: Glitter in 'beautiful big nugget of rhino poo' unlocks fertility clues

children.content: Edible glitter is being used to track the reproductive cycles of the two youngest southern white rhinos Monarto Safari Park.

gallery.altText: A southern white rhino chewing on hay with vivid blue glitter.

thumbnail.altText: A southern white rhino chewing on hay with vivid blue glitter.

items.title: Tribunal upholds three-match ban for Crows forward Darcy Fogarty

children.content: The Crows fail in their challenge to Darcy Fogarty's three-match striking ban at the AFL Tribunal, as the panel finds Fogarty had time to decide to swing his arm at Sydney's Harry Cunningham. Follow live.

image.altText: An Adelaide Crows AFL forward points ahead in celebration after kicking a goal during a game.

items.title: 'Like The Castle': Restaurant owners launch petition against land grab

children.content: The owners of a Mount Barker restaurant have launched a petition to block the government's proposed acquisition of the site on which their business stands.

image.altText: A man stands in front of a artistically graffitied wall with his arms crossed.

items.title: Alleged murderer denies killing woman, says he knew 'something was wrong'

children.content: A man accused of murdering a woman in Adelaide's southern suburbs tells a court he is not to blame for her death, and instead alleges the crime was committed by the victim's ex-wife.

image.altText: A bearded man with a dog.

items.title: Author Randa Abdel-Fattah sues Bendigo Writers Festival organisers

children.content: Randa Abdel-Fattah launches legal action after Bendigo Writers Festival organisers sent a code of conduct that sparked censorship concerns and led to the boycott of dozens of authors.

image.altText: A close-up photo of a woman's face

items.title: Plastic waste burning trial at cement factory prompts backlash

children.content: A major cement manufacturer has been allowed to burn more plastic waste for fuel at its Adelaide site in a decision a local council is seeking to challenge in court.

gallery.altText: A person outside the Adbri cement factory.

thumbnail.altText: A person outside the Adbri cement factory.

items.title: Truck driver reported after concrete slabs fell on major SA road

children.content: A truck driver has been reported over a multi-car crash in which concrete slabs fell onto the North-South Motorway, in Adelaide's north.

image.altText: Large concrete slabs fall from a moving truck on a motorway

items.title: Special guest dazzles aspiring astronauts at Space Day Out

children.content: Young South Australians got one step closer to achieving their space exploration dreams on Sunday when they met a "real-life" astronaut.

image.altText: A lady in a blue astronaut suit looks at the camera with a smile

items.title: SA's largest electorate to be renamed in honour of Aboriginal leader

children.content: South Australia’s largest federal electorate will be renamed in honour of an Aboriginal leader. Experts say it is also the state’s most likely to fall to One Nation.

gallery.altText: a green voting slip is dropped into a voting box

thumbnail.altText: a green voting slip is dropped into a voting box

items.title: Once known as Little Beirut, stories of Adelaide's historical corner uncovered

children.content: It's been a three year labour of love probing into the past and building on the fragments of a hidden history to write Logan St and get it to the stage.

gallery.altText: Alexis West and Ali Ammouchi sit on stage in rehearsals for Logan St.

thumbnail.altText: Alexis West and Ali Ammouchi sit on stage in rehearsals for Logan St.

items.title: 'Right fit': Ken Hinkley announced as inaugural Tasmania Devils coach

children.content: The former Port Adelaide coach is formally announced as the inaugural coach of the Tasmania Devils. It completes a year-long hunt for a senior coach for the AFL's newest club.

image.altText: A man leans against a wooden fence post

items.title: For some in Australia, hope is fading and anger is rising

children.content: Sulaiman Zandinan says Australia is amazing. Wendy Newman says people in the bush are "not country bumpkins". The ABC is inviting you to have your say about the issues, challenges and opportunities that shape your life in 2026.

image.altText: A man with curly hair smiles while holding a sports whistle looking away

items.title: 'Almost certainly new' species of giant earthworms found in SA

children.content: A South Australian ecologist has been tracking giant earthworm sightings for 30 years, despite the species not officially existing in the state.

image.altText: A child holding up a worm which stretches from above his head to his waist

items.title: Uranium export deal sparks talk of opportunities for Broken Hill

children.content: Australia's trade deal to supply India with unenriched uranium sparks conversations about potential new mining opportunities in Broken Hill.

image.altText: An aerial view of a mine in remote Australia.

items.title: Shared lifestyle might be clue to why some couples have higher dementia risk

children.content: Having a spouse with dementia puts you at a higher risk of developing the condition, a new study finds.

items.title: Iran-backed Houthis declare maritime blockade on Saudi Arabia

children.content: In a statement, the Houthis' military spokesperson Yahya Saree says the group will enforce the blockade against its "criminal Saudi enemy", effective immediately.

image.altText: Yahya Sarea stands in a military uniform speaking at a podium.

items.title: US launches new strikes as Trump says Iran will 'pay' for soldiers' deaths

children.content: The United States has launched a new wave of strikes on Iran after identifying two soldiers killed while defending against Iranian missile and drone attacks in Jordan.

image.altText: A black and white image shows an explosion in a landscape, text over it reads "unclassified"

items.title: Firebombed synagogue community to address royal commission

children.content: The community from Melbourne's Adass Israel Synagogue will provide evidence at the Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion today.

image.altText: Three firefighters outside a building which has orange flames coming from the roof at night.

items.title: Israel criticises Labor's draft Palestine policy ahead of party conference

children.content: Israel's embassy has released a lengthy complaint ahead of Labor's national conference on the party's proposed position on Israel and Palestine.

image.altText: A white man with glasses speaks at a lectern in front of a blue background

items.title: Stubborn stigma fuelling shocking violence against sex workers in Australia

children.content: A new inquiry into violence against sex workers in Australia and Thailand comes just weeks after the murder of Thai teenager Tunchanok Donhomla, allegedly by an Australian man.

gallery.altText: a woman carries a sign saying 'stop mythologising men who kill sex workers'
A frame of a young Thai girl looking at the camera.

thumbnail.altText: a woman carries a sign saying 'stop mythologising men who kill sex workers'
A frame of a young Thai girl looking at the camera.

items.title: Anthony Albanese on the night in parliament he would rather not relive

children.content: From left-wing roots to "radical centre" prime minister, Anthony Albanese reflects on his 30-year career in Parliament House.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese stands in front of an Australian flag in a grey suit and blue tie looking serious

items.title: Man charged with murder of UK politician Ann Widdecombe

children.content: Joshua Kerry, 28, will appear in court on Tuesday, UK time, after the death of the prominent conservative politician in her home earlier in July.

image.altText: An older lady speaks to the media.

items.title: The musical journey of royal wedding cellist Sheku Kanneh-Mason

children.content: Sheku Kanneh-Mason went viral performing at Prince Harry and Meghan Markle's wedding. He's captivated audiences since and is touring Australia this month.

gallery.altText: Sheku Kanneh-Mason sits holding a gleaming cello, one hand reaching towards a music stand. There's a grand piano in the room

thumbnail.altText: Sheku Kanneh-Mason sits holding a gleaming cello, one hand reaching towards a music stand. There's a grand piano in the room

items.title: Quiz: How well do you remember these Australian kids' TV shows?

children.content: What was the name of the tough, mangy cat in The Ferals? And which show featured EC, the faceless doll?

image.altText: Child with faceless doll

items.title: Why 'narcissist' bosses are more likely to oppose work from home

children.content: Research published last month found narcissistic leaders — those with a tendency to be self-centred and entitled — were more likely than others to resist remote work. Why? The study concluded "it interferes with their motivations for power and status".

image.altText: a man gesturing as he sits in front of a laptop

items.title: Moscow targeted by drones while Russia bombards Kyiv

children.content: Ukraine launches its latest attack on the Russian capital, firing more than 400 drones, while the Kremlin deploys more ballistic missiles against targets across Ukraine.

gallery.altText: A large smoke plume over a blue sky seen from a suburban street.

thumbnail.altText: A large smoke plume over a blue sky seen from a suburban street.

items.title: India's 'cockroach' movement supporters caned by police

children.content: Thousands of supporters of India's youth-led "cockroach" movement gathered in New Delhi to march ‌on parliament seeking the resignation of the education minister, despite police denying permission for the protest.

image.altText: Crowds of people pushing barricades during a protest at nighttme

items.title: 'Good night, sweet dreams': Derryn Hinch farewelled at state funeral

children.content: Broadcaster Ray Martin, Australian actress Jacki Weaver and performer Rhonda Burchmore are among the entertainment heavyweights to pay tribute to Derryn Hinch at his state funeral service.

image.altText: Justice Party senator Derryn Hinch wears a red ribbon as he speaks to reporters regarding 18c in Parliament House

items.title: Taxpayers fork out almost $35,000 for MPs to stay at Queensland parliament

children.content: It has been revealed in budget estimates that 191 total overnight stays were reported by MPs last financial year, up from just 39 the previous year.

image.altText: Queensland Parliament House in Brisbane through its front fence on George Street.

items.title: Hundreds march on Pine Gap in secretive site's biggest protest in decades

children.content: About 300 people have marched on Pine Gap in Alice Springs, protesting against its role in US military operations, in the largest protest at the top-secret defence site since 1987.

image.altText: A crowd of protesters with police watching them.

items.title: Critical beeding breakthrough welcome news for lovers of purple passionfruit

children.content: An unpalatable future has been averted for Australians who love purple-skinned passionfruit, with new research ensuring better prospects for the delicious fruit.

gallery.altText: Farmer holds open split passionfruit while touching his dog's head.
A smiling mother and her two sons holding passionfruit and a wooden spoon up.

thumbnail.altText: Farmer holds open split passionfruit while touching his dog's head.
A smiling mother and her two sons holding passionfruit and a wooden spoon up.

items.title: Underwater gardening helping to restore Tasmania's lost kelp forests

children.content: A new live-in internship program on the Tasman Peninsula is working to repopulate the area's endangered giant kelp forests through underwater gardening.

image.altText: Video of dappled sunlight shining through giant underwater kelp forest

items.title: Six years out from hosting the Olympics, this is where Brisbane stands

children.content: Broken promises and scarcely broken ground. Five years since Brisbane secured the 2032 Olympic Games, this is the state of play.

image.altText: A building windows lit up in BNE 2032 with fireworks in the sky.

items.title: Quadruplets born in Brisbane 'one of the rarest births in the world'

children.content: A Queensland family has grown by four after an extremely rare pregnancy with identical quadruplets all sharing one placenta.

gallery.altText: An ultrasound image of four fetuses in utero

thumbnail.altText: An ultrasound image of four fetuses in utero
